facts.claim: Buenos Aires, officially the Autonomous City of Buenos Aires, is the capital and largest city of Argentina.
Buenos Aires is classified as an Alpha− global city, according to the GaWC 2024 ranking.

Argentina stretches some 3,800 km north to south and is shaped like an inverted triangle. Its main regions include the Andes, Pampas, Patagonia, and the northern provinces where Iguazú Falls and Quebrada de Humahuaca are found. Spanish is the official language.

Buenos Aires,[c] officially the Autonomous City of Buenos Aires,[d] is the capital and largest city of Argentina. It is located on the southwest of the Río de la Plata. Buenos Aires is classified as an Alpha− global city, according to the GaWC 2024 ranking.[14] The city proper has a population of 3.1 million and its urban area has a population of 16.7 million, making it the 21st most populous metropolitan area in the world.[15]
